resources.string: Granite peaks, ocean shoreline, and woodlands on Maine\'s coast.
Tropical rainforests, coral reefs, and volcanic peaks in the South Pacific.
Over 2,000 natural sandstone arches and unique geological formations.
Striking buttes, pinnacles, and spires mixed with largest undisturbed grass prairie.
Chihuahuan Desert landscapes, Chisos Mountains, and the Rio Grande.
95% underwater; protects coral reefs, mangrove forests, and Florida Keys.
Some of the steepest cliffs and oldest rock in North America.
Giant natural amphitheaters filled with colorful sandstone hoodoos.
A wilderness of countless canyons and fantastically formed buttes.
Protects the Waterpocket Fold, a 100-mile long wrinkle in the Earth\'s crust.
Over 119 caves, including the massive \'Big Room\' limestone chamber.
Five unique islands with endemic species and coastal sea caves.
Largest intact expanse of old-growth bottomland hardwood forest in the SE.
Deepest lake in the USA, formed inside a collapsed volcanic caldera.
Preserves the rural landscape along the Cuyahoga River between cities.
Hottest, driest, and lowest national park; features salt flats and dunes.
Home to North America\'s highest peak and vast subarctic ecosystem.
Remote islands with Fort Jefferson and vibrant coral reefs.
Largest tropical wilderness in the U.S.; critical habitat for rare species.
Entirely north of the Arctic Circle; no roads or established trails.
Iconic 630-foot steel monument symbolizing westward expansion.
\'Crown of the Continent\' with alpine meadows and carved glacial valleys.
Massive tidewater glaciers, ice-capped mountains, and deep fjords.
Immense canyon carved by the Colorado River; world-renowned geology.
Jagged peaks of the Teton Range rising above the Jackson Hole valley.
Ancient bristlecone pines and the marble Lehman Caves.
Tallest sand dunes in North America set against the Sangre de Cristo Mts.
Most visited park; ancient mountains and diverse plant/animal life.
Features the world\'s premier fossil reef and the highest point in Texas.
Dormant volcano with a massive crater and unique silversword plants.
Protects two of the world\'s most active volcanoes: Kīlauea and Mauna Loa.
Historic bathhouses and natural thermal springs in an urban setting.
Rugged dunes, wetlands, and prairies along Lake Michigan\'s shore.
Remote island wilderness in Lake Superior known for wolf/moose studies.
Where the Mojave and Colorado deserts meet; famous for twisted trees.
Famous for brown bears fishing for salmon at Brooks Falls.
Where mountains, ice, and ocean meet; home to the Harding Icefield.
Deep canyons and giant sequoia groves; adjacent to Sequoia NP.
Arctic sand dunes and caribou migration routes; no roads.
Volcanoes, turquoise lakes, and jagged mountains; accessible only by air.
Features all four types of volcanoes found in the world.
The world\'s longest known cave system with over 400 miles explored.
Protects over 5,000 archaeological sites, including cliff dwellings.
Active volcano encased in over 25 glaciers; iconic PNW landmark.
One of the oldest rivers on the continent; famous for whitewater rafting.
Vast wilderness of jagged peaks and over 300 glaciers.
Diverse ecosystems from glacier-capped peaks to temperate rainforests.
Large deposits of petrified wood and the colorful Painted Desert.
Massive monoliths and talus caves formed by an ancient volcano.
Home to the tallest trees on Earth along the Northern California coast.
High-altitude peaks, alpine tundra, and the Continental Divide.
Protects the giant saguaro cactus, a symbol of the American West.
Home to the General Sherman tree, the world\'s largest tree by volume.
Scenic Blue Ridge Mountains and the famous Skyline Drive.
Badlands where the 26th President once ranched; home to bison.
White sand beaches, coral reefs, and historic sugar plantations.
A water-based park of interconnected lakes and boreal forests.
World\'s largest gypsum dunefield; rare white sand landscape.
One of the world\'s longest caves, known for rare \'boxwork\' formations.
Largest U.S. National Park; contains 9 of the 16 highest peaks.
First National Park; famous for geysers, hot springs, and wildlife.
Iconic granite cliffs (El Capitan), waterfalls, and giant sequoias.
Massive sandstone cliffs of cream, pink, and red that soar into the sky.
